rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOROTHY S. RABKIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Elizabeth Rabkin For Reimbursement Of Attorneys Fees And Costs From Trust
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 6th amended general order for information regarding remote appearances at https://www.sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate Issues: a. Petitioner requests if the court orders the reimbursement from Dorothy S. Rabkin Trust of attorney's fees and costs incurred by any of the beneficiaries/co-trustees, Elizabeth Rabkin requests reimbursement of attorney's fees and costs incurred by her in the amount of $102,975.00. b. A hard copy of the proposed order must be dropped off at the Superior Court in room 202. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOROTHY S. RABKIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 6th amended general order for information regarding remote appearances at https://www.sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate Issues: a. In the Petition page 1 Petitioner requests $355,416.12 in attorney's fees and costs plus an amount yet to be determined as "fees for fees." In the request for relief Petitioner requests $340,354.50 in attorney fees and $15,061.62 in costs. b. Petitioner requests $123,289.00 + $34,167.10 be allocated against Robert Rabkin's distributive share. c. Attorney is billing for parking. Parking fees are absorbed as part of fees. (LRSF 14.91.) d. Attorney is billing for research and travel time. This Court does not allow legal fees for routine research and only allows reimbursement for long distance travel depending on the circumstances disclosed. (LRSF 14.91.)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO** CONSERVATORSHIP OF NORMA SHIRAISHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Transfer Of Property Belonging To The Conservatorship Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues: 1) Respondents' failure to deliver courtesy copy of response; 2) Respondent Syed Husain's dual capacity and appearance; and 3) Service of process on Old Republic Title Company. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAY L. BLOOM TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/21/20. (No earlier availability.) There is no detailed schedule of assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period. As all trust petitions are E-filed, subsequent trust accounts should include this detailed schedule of assets on hand at the end of the prior account. After the account is fully reviewed, an appearance will likely be required for the request for fees, which is more than twice the amount requested in prior years. Trustee claims that he has "also rendered all legal services... without any separate claim for attorney compensation", but the time entries, in fact, reflect $3,010.00 for attorney's time, which furthermore includes a lump sum of 5.0 hours between 4/15/20 - 4/30/20, which are not detailed time entries. It is also unclear why the trustee had to personally bring courtesy copies to the court when mailing them would have been less expensive. Bookkeeping time based on an approximation of "15 per hour" is also not sufficiently detailed. (If trustee fees were based on 1% of the FMV of assets on hand at the end of the accounting period, reasonable fees could be $10,944.08 for the two-year period.)

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/31/2020. A Declaration was filed 08/05/2020 and the courtesy copy was received by examiner 2 court days before the hearing. Matter continued to allow time for examiner to review. In order for supplemental or opposition papers to be considered by the Court prior to the hearing, unless otherwise directed by the Court, papers must be filed seven (7) court days before the hearing, and endorsed-filed copies of the papers must be delivered to the Examiners or Investigators five (5) court days before the hearing with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet and any cover letter. [LRSF 14.6 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TRENET CASTLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/20. The amendment was filed on 7/29/20, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Notice of the amendment must be given using the mandatory Judicial Council form, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. [CRC 7.53(a).] Notice of the petition and amendment must be given to the former attorneys (unless waived). (A revised proposed order was just received on 8/5/20. In light of the COVID-19 emergency, coutesy copies, etc., must be delivered to the assigned Examiner at least 10 days before a hearing.) A court-appointed fiduciary is required to sign and verify any supplement or amendment to her own petition. [CRC 7.103(c).] (Declarations by Counsel are unnecessary when a Court-appointed fiduciary petitions and supplements or amends her petition. The verified petition or supplement or amendment stands on its own and does not need further declarations in support and should not refer to Examiner's Notes, which are not filed for the record.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELEANOR B. VINSANT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/14/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 1 of the petition: The date of death is incorrect. 2) Paragraph 6: The alleged claim expiration date is Saturday. See CCP § 12a. 3) Paragraph 10(a): Was the notice given to the DHCS because it was required? 4) Paragraph 10(c): i) What was the manner of giving notice to the FTB? ii) Why was the notice given after the 90-day statutory deadline? 5) Paragraph 15 (& accounting schedules): A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. LRSF 14.59. 6) Paragraph 27: The amount stated following "1% of next" is incorrect. 7) Paragraph 30(a): The airfare expenses were incurred prior to the decedent's death. Why are they reimbursable expenses of administration? 8) Paragraph 30(b): The total amount of post & parcels is incorrect. 9) Paragraph 30(c): Did the petitioner file a creditor claim for the funeral expenses and obtain court approval? Probate Code §§ 9000(a)(3) & 9252. See also LRSF 14.49. 10) i) Paragraph 30(d): Did the petitioner give notice of proposed action before donating the personal property? §§ 10510 & 10513. ii) The inventories and appraisals on file do not show the donated property and its appraised value. 11) Paragraph 35: § 9650 only refers to real property and tangible personal property. Why did the petitioner make a preliminary cash distribution to Donna Francesconi? 12) Schedule B: How are the receipts described as "Pacific Life" and "Palac" annuities related to the inventoried retirement accounts? 13) Schedule D: i) The first two disbursement items predate the issuance of letters. What was the source of those payments? ii) Schedule D: What was the purpose of the social work case management payment to Jewish Family and Children Services on 11/23/2018? iii) The court's record shows $127.50 paid for 6 certified copies of letters on 11/23/2018. iv) Did Omnicare, Kaiser, Northstar Emergency Services, Rhonda Goldman Plaza, and King-American Ambulance present claims to the petitioner? v) Is the year of the $75 payment to the referee accurate? vi) What were the purposes of payments of "orphan & abandon fees" to Pershing LLC? 14) Schedule G: Copies of lump-sum distribution checks for the listed 4 annuities must be filed. 15) Schedule H: i) Account statements verifying the ending values must be filed. LRSF 14.62 & 14.93D. ii) Why is the estate cash exceeding the FDIC limits held by one financial institution? 16) Schedules C, E, H-1, & I-1: i) Some shares were purchased and sold before letters were issued. Who authorized those transactions? ii) Are the funds purchased by the petitioner authorized investments under § 10533? 17) Paragraph 1 of Amendment: The summary of account does not match the first page of Exhibit 1. 18) Exhibit 1 to Amendment: A schedule of liabilities is missing. § 1063(g). 19) Exhibit 1 to Declaration: Account statements verifying the ending values (Schedule H) must be filed. 20) Proof of Service of Notice of Hearing: i) Donna Francesconi's zip code and Lu Chaikin's apartment number are different from those listed in papers previously filed. ii) Is Linda Heese the same person as Linda Hesse named in the will?

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues: 1) Small estate affidavit; and 2) Fees of petitioner and attorney. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ILENE BRETHOLZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/20. The date of death of Dora (Brettholz) Walton was not set forth. The addresses of the heirs are not set forth in the [second] verified supplement filed on 7/30/20. If Alan Dickstein postdeceased on 11/10/19, did he die testate or intestate? His surviving children are not necessarily his successors-in-interest. Is there no formal estate proceeding for him? Did he have any children who did not survive him? (Not having been survived by "children" does not necessarily mean that no issue survived.) A proof of service filed on 7/15/20 was verified on 6/15/20 but indicates that notice was mailed on 7/15/20, which would make the verification untrue. Notice was reportedly mailed to Sidnie Heiberger, but Sidnie Brettholz was identified as an heir. James Brett was identified as an heir, but notice was mailed to James Brettholz. (Notice of Petition to Administer Estate does not need to be given to the Court; in any event, no Notice has been received here to this Examiner's knowledge.) Courtesy copies of a third supplement, signed and verified by the petitioner, and a correct, complete proof of service must be delivered to the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/14/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN HENRY MCCOY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/24/2020. 1) Declinations signed by the decedent's three other siblings having equal priority over appointment are not on file. If such declinations cannot be obtained, the petitioner must explain the reason. LRSF 14.33. 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The decedent's second aka name included in the caption of the petition is incomplete. B) Is the personal property identified at item 3d of the petition cash or other liquid asset? If so, why is the probably annual income $0? C) Item 6b of the petition was not checked, even though item 8 alleges that the decedent's siblings are the heirs. D) What are the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents? E) There was no Attachment 8, even though items 8 & 9 mention a one-page attachment.
